step_by_step_thinking:
Agree with the initial assessment that the diameter of a nerve fiber primarily influences conduction velocity because larger fibers have less membrane capacitance per unit length which allows for faster propagation of action potentials compared to smaller ones. This is supported by both physiological principles and experimental evidence showing direct correlation between axon size and speed of impulse transmission.

Agree. My additional point reinforces how increased cross-sectional area directly correlates with reduced time required for an electrical signal to travel along the neuron, thus confirming option A as correct based on established neurophysiology.
